title: Alex Cora reshuffles Red Sox lineup, drops Trevor Story out of No. 2 spot
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Alex Cora shuffled the Red Sox lineup, moving Trevor Story to the No. 5 spot due to a slow start.
Masataka Yoshida will bat second, while Willson Contreras and Wilyer Abreu take the No. 3 and No. 4 spots.
Boston, now 2-7, struggles offensively, ranking 26th in MLB for walk percentage and fifth in strikeout percentage.

title: Cleveland radio host says Cavaliers are ‘scared’ of facing the Celtics in the playoffs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cleveland Cavaliers currently hold the fourth seed, potentially avoiding a second-round playoff clash with Boston Celtics.
ESPN Cleveland host Andrew Goldhammer claims Cavaliers prefer 4th seed to evade Celtics due to fear.
Cavs face injuries with Evan Mobley, Jarrett Allen, and others missing games; regular-season ends with four games left.

title: Where you live in Mass. can shape your electric bill — is yours getting out of control?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts residents face high electricity costs, with locally-owned municipal utilities often charging half of investor-owned companies.
Municipal utilities serve 13% of Massachusetts energy customers, benefiting from lower service costs and stable power contracts.
Switching to municipal utilities is costly, with municipal aggregation offering a feasible alternative for many communities.

title: Northeastern students rally over lack of communication after recent violence
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Northeastern students rallied for improved communication after recent violence near campus, including a fatal police shooting.
Students criticized the university for its delayed and insufficient updates, turning instead to news outlets for incident information.
University claimed increased patrols and coordination with Boston police, acknowledging concerns about incidents' proximity to campus.

title: BU president pauses signage policy after Pride flag controversy
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: BU President Melissa Gilliam pauses the signage policy after controversy over the removal of Pride flags on campus.
The policy restricted outward-facing materials, prompting protests and a petition from BU's campus community seeking reconsideration.
Gilliam acknowledges the impact on LGBTQ+ students and calls for further dialogue to address community concerns and values.

title: Red Sox complete Sonny Gray trade, send pitching prospect to Cardinals
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Boston Red Sox finalized the trade for Sonny Gray by sending pitcher Patrick Galle to the St. Louis Cardinals.
Patrick Galle, a 22-year-old right-handed pitcher, was drafted from Ole Miss and played with Single A affiliate Salem.
Sonny Gray, a three-time All-Star, has a 4.50 ERA this season with the Red Sox and is scheduled to pitch against Milwaukee.

title: ‘The Real Housewives of Rhode Island’: Episode 2 recap
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Episode 2 of 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' aired on its new Sunday time-slot, featuring escalating local drama.
Ashley Iaconetti shares concerns over stressful business and motherhood during a playdate with Jo-Ellen Tiberi in the park.
Rulla Nehme Pontarelli confronts rumors about her husband's affair spread by Jo-Ellen Tiberi, fueling tension among the cast.

title: Mass. House to vote on youth social media ban, strict school cellphone ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts House to vote on banning social media use for children under 14 and requiring parental consent for ages 14-15.
Proposed bill includes a ban on student cellphone use during school hours, with guidance for implementation.
Quincy schools plan stricter cellphone policy using secure pouches to reduce classroom distractions.

title: Maine father dies after saving his children from a rip current
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Ryan Jennings, 46, died after saving his children from a rip current at Juno Beach, Florida, on April 1, 2026.
The Palm Beach County Fire Department reported the Jennings family was swimming in an area without lifeguards during the incident.
A GoFundMe page for Jennings' family raised over $170,000, highlighting his heroic actions during the incident.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon to support neglected and at-risk kids
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Sean Meehan, 28, from Natick, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for The Home For Little Wanderers.
Meehan was inspired to run after watching his sister finish the race in 2023.
The Home For Little Wanderers aids neglected and at-risk children through foster care, education, and health services.
